definition: Fiscal deficits raise the term premium on long-duration government bonds through a supply channel. The relationship is non-linear: insensitive below ~3-4% structural deficit for a reserve currency issuer, but highly sensitive above that threshold. Central estimate: 20-40bp per 1pp deficit/GDP in pre-QE samples, but with ±20bp confidence band and a structural break around QE regimes.
evidence: When net private-sector absorption >4% GDP, term premium >100bp in 6/7 episodes (86%)
UK Truss 2022: ~100bp spike in 4 trading days from fiscal shock
2023-24 Treasury maturity shift: 30-50bp compression from supply management
ACM term premium swing from -50bp (2020) to +50-100bp (early 2026)
open_questions: What is the precise magnitude under current QT regime (sample too short)?
Does the U.S. reserve currency status permanently lower the elasticity?
At what point does further deficit expansion weaken rather than strengthen the dollar?
